codeReview.summary: Feature implementation is complete and meets all requirements. The worker discovered that the aggressive pipelining requested in the feature was already implemented via double-buffer + stream overlap infrastructure, then created a comprehensive benchmark to measure the benefit. Results show 48.1% throughput improvement (21.8 → 32.2 tok/s) with 0.999990 cosine similarity, far exceeding the 2% improvement target.

issues.description: Benchmark creates two separate TP engines (tp_std and tp_db) which requires loading model weights twice. This increases memory pressure and test time, but is acceptable for a benchmark that runs infrequently.

The analysis notes 'Expected real speedup: 2-5%' but measured 48.1%. The explanation for this discrepancy is speculative. Could benefit from deeper investigation into why standard overlap is so much slower, but this is beyond the scope of the current feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from 'Write Tests First (RED)' step in gpu-kernel-worker skill. This was justified because the feature infrastructure already existed - the double-buffer implementation was complete. Worker created benchmark for validation rather than new implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations: 'Created benchmark directly since the implementation already exists' - The feature description asked to 'explore' and 'measure', not implement new code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The double-buffer implementation in _decode_step_cached_stream() is a well-established pattern. Other workers should be aware this path exists for compute-communication overlap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/tp_engine.py lines 2947-3128 show comprehensive double-buffer support with buffer swap logic. The existing test_double_buffer_tp4.py and test_overlap_double_buffer.py validate this functionality.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The benchmark demonstrates a significant performance gap between standard stream overlap (21.8 tok/s) and double-buffer overlap (32.2 tok/s). This 48% improvement is much larger than the theoretical 2-5% expectation, suggesting the standard path has overhead issues worth investigating.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: bench/M2_PIPELINE_OVERLAP_ANALYSIS.md lines 84-95 document this discrepancy. The standard path appears to have suboptimal event synchronization or CPU-blocking behavior.

summary: PASS - Feature fully meets requirements. Worker correctly identified that aggressive pipelining was already implemented via double-buffer + stream overlap. Created comprehensive benchmark (tests/bench_m2_pipeline_overlap.py) with thorough documentation (bench/M2_PIPELINE_OVERLAP_ANALYSIS.md). Measured 48.1% throughput improvement (exceeds 2% target) with 0.999990 cosine similarity (exceeds 0.99 threshold). Documented that overlap is absolutely worth the complexity. Skill deviation from TDD was justified since feature was about exploring/measuring existing infrastructure, not implementing new code.
